skill.name: scroll-experience
skill.description: Expert in building immersive scroll-driven experiences with parallax storytelling, scroll animations, interactive narratives, and cinematic web experiences like NY Times interactives and Apple product pages.
skill.summary: Expert in building immersive scroll-driven web experiences with parallax effects and animations
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a front-end development resource for scroll-driven web experiences. Static findings flagged 'external_commands' (backtick execution) and 'weak cryptographic algorithms' - both are FALSE POSITIVES. The backticks are markdown code fence delimiters, not shell commands. The crypto detections are substring matches in words like 'experiences' and 'interactive'. No actual security risks exist. This skill only provides JavaScript, React, and CSS code examples for scroll animations.

content.value_statement: Create cinematic scroll-driven web experiences with parallax storytelling and engaging animations that capture user attention from first scroll.
actual_capabilities: Implement scroll-triggered animations using GSAP ScrollTrigger, Framer Motion, or native CSS
Create parallax storytelling experiences with multiple depth layers
Build sticky sections that pin content during scroll
Design horizontal scroll sections within vertical scroll
Add text reveal animations including fade-in, typewriter, and word-by-word highlights
Optimize scroll performance to prevent jank and stuttering
limitations: Does not provide actual implementation code for complex animations - only guidance and patterns
Does not include pre-built animation libraries or components
No backend integration or data fetching capabilities
Accessibility considerations are discussed but not enforced

use_cases.title: Landing Page Designer
use_cases.description: Create stunning product landing pages with scroll-driven reveals that guide users through features progressively
use_cases.target_user: Web designers and front-end developers building marketing sites

use_cases.title: Interactive Storyteller
use_cases.description: Build narrative experiences where scrolling advances a story with parallax backgrounds and animated text
use_cases.target_user: Creative developers and digital agencies creating immersive content

use_cases.title: Portfolio Enhancer
use_cases.description: Transform personal portfolios into engaging experiences that showcase work through scroll-triggered animations
use_cases.target_user: Freelance developers and designers showcasing projects

prompt_templates.title: Basic Scroll Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a simple scroll-triggered animation for my landing page. When users scroll to a section, elements should fade in and move up. Use GSAP ScrollTrigger with a smooth scrub effect.

prompt_templates.title: Parallax Hero Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hero section with parallax depth. I want multiple layers moving at different speeds: background at 0.2x, midground at 0.5x, and foreground at normal speed. The content should remain readable.

prompt_templates.title: Sticky Story Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a sticky section where text stays pinned on the left while images scroll on the right. Use GSAP to pin for 1000px and animate content sequentially as user scrolls.

prompt_templates.title: Horizontal Scroll Gallery
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert vertical scroll into horizontal movement for an image gallery. When users scroll past a trigger point, the experience should lock into horizontal scroll mode until the gallery completes.

output_examples.input: Create a parallax effect for my product page hero
output_examples.output: A hero section with three depth layers: background image at 0.2x scroll speed, product visual at 0.5x, and headline text at normal speed. Use CSS sticky positioning for the container and GSAP for smooth parallax movement.
Text elements that animate in using a combination of opacity and transform. Use scroll position to trigger: 0% shows first word highlighted, 50% shows second, with smooth transitions between states.

A pinned section where each step fades in sequentially as the user scrolls. Steps are centered with a progress indicator showing current position. Uses GSAP ScrollTrigger with markers for precise timing.

best_practices: Start with mobile-first design - test scroll interactions on touch devices before adding desktop complexity
Use CSS scroll-timeline for simple animations to reduce JavaScript dependency and improve performance
Keep animation durations under 300ms for scroll-linked animations to maintain responsiveness
Always provide fallback static content for users who prefer reduced motion
anti_patterns: Scroll hijacking - never override native scroll behavior completely, users lose control and become frustrated
Animation overload - too many moving elements distract from content and hurt performance
Desktop-only experiences - mobile users represent the majority and deserve the same quality experience
Ignoring accessibility - ensure scroll animations respect prefers-reduced-motion and are keyboard navigable

faq.question: What libraries work best for scroll animations?
faq.answer: GSAP ScrollTrigger is the most powerful for complex animations. Framer Motion is ideal for React projects. Lenis provides smooth scroll with minimal setup. CSS scroll-timeline is best for simple native animations.

faq.question: How do I prevent scroll jank and stuttering?
faq.answer: Use will-change sparingly, avoid animating layout properties like width and height, prefer transform and opacity, and use requestAnimationFrame. Test on actual mobile devices during development.

faq.question: Can scroll animations work on mobile?
faq.answer: Yes, but keep effects simpler. Touch scroll has different timing than mouse wheel. Use libraries like Lenis for smooth scrolling and test extensively on real devices.

faq.question: How do I handle users who prefer reduced motion?
faq.answer: Check window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') and provide static fallbacks. Disable scrub animations and reduce animation duration for these users.

faq.question: What is the difference between parallax and scroll-triggered animations?
faq.answer: Parallax creates depth by moving layers at different speeds simultaneously. Scroll-triggered animations fire at specific scroll positions. They can be combined for rich experiences.

faq.question: How do I integrate scroll animations with existing frameworks?
faq.answer: Most scroll libraries work with any framework. For React, Framer Motion has built-in scroll hooks. For Vue, consider ScrollMagic or custom composables. Vanilla JavaScript works everywhere.
